data.title: HSBC Plans Risk Transfer Tied to $2.3 Billion of Corporate Loans, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
--HSBC Holdings plans a significant risk transfer linked to about 2 billion euros ($2.33 billion) of corporate loans, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.

--A representative for HSBC declined to comment, according to Bloomberg.

CDP and Intesa, EUR1 Billion to Boost Growth for SMEs and Mid-Caps
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Intesa Sanpaolo Spa and Cassa Depositi e Prestiti Spa announced on Wednesday that they have signed a new EUR1 billion agreement to support access to credit and market expansion for micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises, while also fostering the real economy and the development of the regions in which they operate.

As the companies explained in a statement, the initiative is part of an ongoing collaboration aimed at promoting projects for Italian businesses which, since 2021, have made available a total of around EUR5 billion to support the growth of more than 6,000 companies.

Specifically, the funds will be fully used by the bank to provide loans of up to EUR25 million, with terms of up to 18 years, to Italian SMEs and Mid-Caps for individual projects.

"The resources may be allocated to investments to be made or already underway to strengthen the main national production chains, to expenditures for tangible or intangible assets, and to working capital needs," the statement reads.

Intesa Sanpaolo is trading slightly higher at EUR5.99 per share.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

Billionbrains Garage Ventures Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Billionbrains Garage Ventures Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported revenue was INR 12,610.72 million compared to INR 10,044.47 million a year ago. Net income was INR 5,469.33 million compared to INR 7,571.1 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.89 compared to INR 1.38 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.87 compared to INR 1.32 a year ago.
For the nine months, revenue was INR 32,803.41 million compared to INR 32,120.73 million a year ago. Net income was INR 13,966.46 million compared to INR 15,152.84 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 2.35 compared to INR 2.81 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 2.29 compared to INR 2.68 a year ago.

data.title: Ryanair to cut Brussels Charleroi capacity due to passenger tax
data.description:
BRUSSELS, Jan 14 (Reuters) - Ryanair plans to operate around 10% fewer flights from Brussels Charleroi Airport due to a new 3 euro ($3.50) passenger tax taking effect from April, Europe's largest low-cost carrier said on Wednesday.

The Irish airline will cut the number of seats from flights landing or taking off from Charleroi by 1.1 million from its current annual total of 10.5 million, Group Chief Executive Michael O'Leary told journalists.

"We will keep cutting until the Belgian government ... abolishes these stupid taxes," O'Leary said.

($1 = 0.8584 euros)

(Reporting by Julia Payne. Writing by Conor Humphries. Editing by Mark Potter)

European Midday Briefing : Stocks and Precious Metals Rise; Traders Await U.S. Economic Data -2-
data.description:
WASHINGTON-A Justice Department memo asserted that the support of the Venezuelan opposition led by Maria Corina Machado for U.S. action to oust Nicolás Maduro helped President Trump's legal case to overthrow him, people familiar with the matter said.

The Dec. 23, 2025, opinion from the Office of Legal Counsel also stated U.S. or international law didn't constrain Trump from ordering the Jan. 3 military operation, citing numerous instances of presidents authorizing missions without congressional approval or United Nations Security Council resolutions.

U.S. to Unveil Gaza Governance Plan Despite Concerns Over Hamas

WASHINGTON-The Trump administration will announce Wednesday the U.S. is moving to the next step of its Gaza plan and name a committee of Palestinians to temporarily run the bombed-out enclave, U.S. officials said.

Since a fragile cease-fire in Gaza is holding and Hamas has returned all but one deceased hostage, U.S. officials assess that they can now shift from halting the conflict to governing and rebuilding the territory, which the administration labels Phase 2 of its 20-point blueprint.

Trump Predicts Strong Economic Growth in 2026 During Speech in Detroit

DETROIT-President Trump said the U.S. economy was poised for solid growth this year, pointing to steadying inflation data as he sought to address one of Republicans' biggest vulnerabilities ahead of this year's midterm elections.

Traveling to Michigan, a battleground state, Trump sought to showcase his manufacturing and trade agenda with an address to the Detroit Economic Club and a visit to a Ford Motor plant in Dearborn, Mich., where the automaker produces the strong-selling F-150 pickup.
